Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), with mild hyperchloremia and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, glucosuria, ketonuria, proteinuria). Liver and electrolyte parameters are within normal limits. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, possibly with nephrotic-range proteinuria and glycosuria; further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
